function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/tmp/patch_netlist.py", "file_text": """"
Surgically remove reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h and reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h from the
synthesis netlist so GlobalPlacement can proceed without re-running synthesis.
"""
import re, shutil

nl_path = '/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v2/03-yosys-synthesis/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.nl.v'

shutil.copy2(nl_path, nl_path + '.bak')

with open(nl_path) as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

PORTS_TO_REMOVE = {'reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h', 'reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h'}

new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    # Lines 135-138: remove standalone inout/wire declarations
    stripped = line.strip().rstrip(';').strip()
    if stripped in (f'inout {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    if stripped in (f'wire {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    new_lines.append(line)

# Line 4: remove from port list (comma-separated on one line)
result = []
for line in new_lines:
    # Check if it's the port list line containing one of our ports
    if any(p in line for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE:
            # Remove ", port_name" or "port_name, " patterns
            line = re.sub(r',\s*' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\s*,\s*', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
    result.append(line)

with open(nl_path, 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(result)

# Verify
with open(nl_path) as f:
    content = f.read()

remaining = sum(1 for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE if p in content)
print(f"Remaining occurrences: {remaining} (should be 0)")

# Confirm lines 133-140 look clean
lines2 = content.split('\n')
for i, ln in enumerate(lines2[132:142], start=133):
    print(f"{i}: {ln}")
"}
function.name: str_replace_editor

args.file_text:
"""
Surgically remove reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h and reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h from the
synthesis netlist so GlobalPlacement can proceed without re-running synthesis.
"""
import re, shutil

nl_path = '/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v2/03-yosys-synthesis/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.nl.v'

shutil.copy2(nl_path, nl_path + '.bak')

with open(nl_path) as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

PORTS_TO_REMOVE = {'reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h', 'reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h'}

new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    # Lines 135-138: remove standalone inout/wire declarations
    stripped = line.strip().rstrip(';').strip()
    if stripped in (f'inout {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    if stripped in (f'wire {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    new_lines.append(line)

# Line 4: remove from port list (comma-separated on one line)
result = []
for line in new_lines:
    # Check if it's the port list line containing one of our ports
    if any(p in line for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE:
            # Remove ", port_name" or "port_name, " patterns
            line = re.sub(r',\s*' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\s*,\s*', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
    result.append(line)

with open(nl_path, 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(result)

# Verify
with open(nl_path) as f:
    content = f.read()

remaining = sum(1 for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE if p in content)
print(f"Remaining occurrences: {remaining} (should be 0)")

# Confirm lines 133-140 look clean
lines2 = content.split('\n')
for i, ln in enumerate(lines2[132:142], start=133):
    print(f"{i}: {ln}")

extras.new_content:
"""
Surgically remove reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h and reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h from the
synthesis netlist so GlobalPlacement can proceed without re-running synthesis.
"""
import re, shutil

nl_path = '/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v2/03-yosys-synthesis/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.nl.v'

shutil.copy2(nl_path, nl_path + '.bak')

with open(nl_path) as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

PORTS_TO_REMOVE = {'reset_n_pad_a_esd_0_h', 'reset_n_pad_a_noesd_h'}

new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    # Lines 135-138: remove standalone inout/wire declarations
    stripped = line.strip().rstrip(';').strip()
    if stripped in (f'inout {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    if stripped in (f'wire {p}' for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        continue
    new_lines.append(line)

# Line 4: remove from port list (comma-separated on one line)
result = []
for line in new_lines:
    # Check if it's the port list line containing one of our ports
    if any(p in line for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE):
        for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE:
            # Remove ", port_name" or "port_name, " patterns
            line = re.sub(r',\s*' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\s*,\s*', '', line)
            line = re.sub(r'\b' + re.escape(p) + r'\b', '', line)
    result.append(line)

with open(nl_path, 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(result)

# Verify
with open(nl_path) as f:
    content = f.read()

remaining = sum(1 for p in PORTS_TO_REMOVE if p in content)
print(f"Remaining occurrences: {remaining} (should be 0)")

# Confirm lines 133-140 look clean
lines2 = content.split('\n')
for i, ln in enumerate(lines2[132:142], start=133):
    print(f"{i}: {ln}")
